understanding.goal_understanding: Each level’s objective was to place all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked with a “+”, with the requirement that every goal tile be occupied by a piece at the same time for the level to register WIN (and finishing the final WIN ends the whole run/game).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A single piece is “selected” at a time (the log always referred to “Selected piece”). CLICK actions attempt to move the currently selected piece to the clicked grid position; if the destination/path is invalid (e.g., blocked by walls/obstacles, not reachable under the movement rule, or occupied in a non-allowed way), the piece does not move and the turn is still consumed. In the turns shown, clicking [9,21] and [18,21] repeatedly moved the selected piece back and forth between those two coordinates, suggesting CLICK is a direct move to a reachable target cell (likely constrained by line-of-sight or corridor connectivity), while many other CLICKs produced no movement because they were blocked/unreachable. I did not observe any successful pushing: when movement was blocked, the piece simply stayed in place.

orient_history: Moves (UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT) attempt to translate the selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) but are prevented by wall collisions. CLICK likely either re-selects a piece or directly places/teleports the selected piece to the clicked location if valid; since movement is impossible, testing CLICK-to-place on the goal is the best hypothesis.

CLICK likely changes which selectable piece is controlled/selected; MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) if not blocked by walls/other collidables. Since the current piece is fully boxed in, we should switch to the other selectable and try moving it toward the goal region.

Hypothesis: Arrow moves slide the selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) but cannot pass through walls. CLICK on another selectable switches selection; CLICK on an empty/goal tile attempts to move the currently selected piece there if some movement/line-of-sight/path rule allows it. Since the current piece is immobile by arrows, we should try switching selection to see if the other piece has a viable click-move path toward the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts to slide the currently selected 3x3 piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) but is blocked by adjacent walls/objects. CLICK on another selectable changes selection to that piece; CLICK on non-selectables has no effect.

Hypothesis: CLICK can reposition the selected piece directly to the clicked coordinate when allowed by the level rules (possibly teleport-to-target or swap/interaction-based movement). MOVE_* is standard 1-cell movement but currently blocked. Trying a CLICK on the goal may place the piece onto the goal (or reveal why it’s blocked).

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a selectable if clicked on it; goals are not selectable. MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) and is blocked by collidable walls. We likely need to select the other piece to find a movable path toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects/switches between selectable pieces; MOVE_* attempts 1-cell (3px) movement but may be possible from the other selectable even if current one is boxed. Need to find a movable piece to reach the goal tile at [35,17].

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable position switches/teleports the controllable piece to that selectable node; after switching, movement options may differ by node. Need to find a node from which movement toward the goal is possible.

MOVE_* cannot be used (fully enclosed). Progress likely requires CLICK-based placement/teleport; the earlier miss on [35,17] may be due to needing to click within the goal’s interior/center rather than its top-left pixel.

Movement via MOVE_* is blocked at [9,21], so progression likely relies on CLICK-based relocation (possibly swapping/teleporting between selectable nodes). Need to relocate to a position with unblocked MOVE paths toward the goal, starting by clicking the other selectable at [18,21].

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to move/interact at the target cell; it may be blocked by walls, but clicking specific wall tiles might toggle/remove them (or otherwise open a path). Since direct clicking into the goal area had no effect, we need to test whether walls are destructible/interactive via CLICK.

Movement (MOVE_*) is currently impossible due to surrounding walls. CLICK might allow teleporting the selected 3x3 piece to an empty cell if the click lands on a valid cell origin (x,y multiples of 3). Prior failed goal clicks may have been invalid because they were not aligned to cell origins.

Hypothesis: CLICK only works to switch/swap between selectables; movement via MOVE_* depends on the currently selected piece’s local free space (may differ for the other selectable). Need to select the other piece to test if it has any available movement toward the goal region.

Hypothesis: CLICK may move the selected piece to the clicked destination only when the click is on a valid, grid-aligned (multiple-of-3) coordinate inside a non-collidable area (like the goal). Previous goal click at [35,17] might have failed because it is not aligned to the 3-pixel cell grid, even though it’s within the goal object.

CLICK likely switches which selectable is controlled (the UI reports it as the selected piece 'moving' to the clicked selectable's position). MOVE_* then attempts to move the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 px) but is blocked by walls. We need to find a selectable that has at least one unblocked move path toward the goal area.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects the piece at the clicked position (the 'selected piece position' changes accordingly); arrow moves attempt to step 1 cell (3 pixels) but are blocked by walls. We may need to select the other piece to find a movable one or reveal a hidden corridor not apparent from the sampled semantic row.

Hypothesis: CLICK can either (a) switch control between selectables, or (b) attempt to move the currently selected selectable to the clicked location if that location is a valid target (likely requires exact object anchor/inside-hitbox). Since the goal pocket is isolated by walls, winning likely depends on a special click interaction with the goal object’s anchor rather than grid-path movement.

CLICK on a selectable likely changes which piece is selected (log reports it as a 'move'); movement options may differ depending on which selectable is selected, so we should swap selection and reassess possible moves toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK may interact with certain objects (possibly walls/doors) even though MOVE is blocked. The central wall rectangle (wall_24_12) might be toggleable; if removed/opened it could create a path to the goal region.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches selection (does not physically move pieces). Arrow MOVE actions move only the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 px), but a piece in a 1-cell pocket is fully immobile. Next I should verify whether the other selectable has any mobility or triggers different interactions when selected.

CLICK on a selectable changes the selected 'player' piece (no actual movement). Arrow moves attempt to slide the selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell), but only if not blocked by walls. Need to find which selectable has a path to the goal area, then move it onto the goal; if multiple goals, may need both pieces placed simultaneously.

Hypothesis: CLICK only works if it hits a valid interactive area; the goal may require clicking within its interior (not the exact top-left corner) to trigger a teleport/move. Movement keys are currently irrelevant due to wall enclosure.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches the active piece; CLICK on certain special tiles (possibly exact goal-cell anchors) may teleport/place the active selectable there, but our previous goal clicks may have missed the required anchor pixel/cell. Since movement is impossible, solving likely requires a precise CLICK target in the goal region to place/activate a piece onto the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK primarily changes which selectable is active (possibly only works when clicking directly on a selectable). Arrow moves attempt to move the active piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) but are blocked by walls. Next step is to switch selection and test interactions from the other piece (in case only one can be moved/placed onto the goal).

CLICK likely changes which selectable is controlled (and/or attempts a line-of-sight move); to progress we should control the other selectable and check if it has any available movement lanes toward the goal region.

CLICK likely attempts to move/teleport the selected piece to the exact clicked pixel cell, but it seems to require grid alignment (multiples of 3). Earlier successful CLICKs were on aligned positions like [9,21] and [18,21]. The goal at [35,17] is off-grid, so we should CLICK an aligned pixel inside the goal’s area (e.g., [36,18]) to land on it.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to reposition the currently-selected selectable directly to the clicked pixel location (no step-by-step path), but only succeeds if the destination is a non-wall cell (goals are non-collidable). Prior failed clicks were likely into wall cells or invalid destinations; so clicking a goal cell on the same y should work.

CLICK likely has a special interaction (not normal movement), possibly selecting/swapping pieces when clicking their cells and/or teleporting to a goal only when clicking the exact goal object position (not just any + semantic cell). Since MOVE is fully blocked, the remaining viable path is an exact-goal CLICK interaction.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active (and/or swaps), and movement to the goal may require having the correct selectable selected before clicking an aligned goal position (likely [36,18] to fit a 3x3 inside the 5x5 goal).

Hypothesis: CLICK can relocate the selected piece only to specific valid anchor pixels (likely aligned to 3-pixel cell grid). The goal covers multiple anchor cells; we may have missed the exact anchor pixel that triggers placement onto the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* only works if adjacent cell is empty (not wall). CLICK appears to switch/move the currently selected selectable onto another selectable’s position (works between [9,21] and [18,21]); clicks onto non-selectable tiles (including the goal area) have no effect, likely because they are blocked/unreachable in this layout. With 1 step left, only a CLICK that changes state is possible.

Pieces move via CLICK-to-teleport/slide to the clicked location only if a valid path/placement exists; otherwise no movement occurs. With 0 steps remaining, further actions likely have no effect and the level cannot be completed now.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Each level’s objective was to place all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked with a “+”, with the requirement that every goal tile be occupied by a piece at the same time for the level to register WIN (and finishing the final WIN ends the whole run/game).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A single piece is “selected” at a time (the log always referred to “Selected piece”). CLICK actions attempt to move the currently selected piece to the clicked grid position; if the destination/path is invalid (e.g., blocked by walls/obstacles, not reachable under the movement rule, or occupied in a non-allowed way), the piece does not move and the turn is still consumed. In the turns shown, clicking [9,21] and [18,21] repeatedly moved the selected piece back and forth between those two coordinates, suggesting CLICK is a direct move to a reachable target cell (likely constrained by line-of-sight or corridor connectivity), while many other CLICKs produced no movement because they were blocked/unreachable. I did not observe any successful pushing: when movement was blocked, the piece simply stayed in place.
